They compare them to the Sacagawea Golden Dollar, stating that the Presidential 1 coins are the same size and color. However, there's an important difference: for the first time since the 1930s, there's an inscription on the edge. Looking at the options: A) There are no words on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The passage says that the new coins have inscriptions on the edge for the first time since the 1930s. The Sacagawea Golden Dollar is mentioned as being similar in size and color but with an important difference. If the difference is the inscription on the edge, then perhaps the Sacagawea coin does not have that. But does that mean there are no words on it? Well, coins usually have some inscriptions. The passage doesn't explicitly say the Sacagawea has no words, but the implication is that the new coins have an additional inscription on the edge that the Sacagawea doesn't have. C) The new coin takes on a white color. The passage says the new coins are gold-colored, same as the Sacagawea. So C is incorrect. D) The Sacagawea Golden Dollar is bigger. The passage says the Presidential 1 coins are the same size as the Sacagawea. So D is incorrect. Therefore, the correct answer is A.
